title: Namespaces: Isolating teams and environments
scenario: Your platform team wants a dedicated `staging` namespace for the QA team. Create the namespace, then deploy a `frontend` Deployment (2 replicas) and a ClusterIP Service in it — isolated from the `default` namespace.
starterYaml:
apiVersion: v1
kind: Namespace
metadata:
  name: staging
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: frontend
  namespace: staging
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: frontend
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: frontend
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: web
        image: nginx:1.25
        ports:
        - containerPort: 80
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: frontend-svc
  namespace: staging
spec:
  type: NodePort
  selector:
    app: web
  ports:
  - port: 80
    targetPort: 80

objectives.description: Service routes to at least 1 Running pod in `staging`

hints.text: All three resources (Namespace, Deployment, Service) must set `metadata.namespace: staging`. The Namespace resource itself does not need a namespace field.
The Service selector must match the Deployment pod labels exactly: `app: frontend`.
Resources in `staging` are invisible to workloads in `default`. This is the isolation Kubernetes namespaces provide.

solution:
apiVersion: v1
kind: Namespace
metadata:
  name: staging
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: frontend
  namespace: staging
spec:
  replicas: 2
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: frontend
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: frontend
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: web
        image: nginx:1.25
        ports:
        - containerPort: 80
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: frontend-svc
  namespace: staging
spec:
  type: ClusterIP
  selector:
    app: frontend
  ports:
  - port: 80
    targetPort: 80
